title: South Korea commits $880 billion over 10 years to AI, chips, and robotics
subtitle: President Lee Jae-myung's plan pairs new Samsung and SK fabs with 8.4 gigawatts of data-center capacity by 2029 — the largest single national semiconductor push on record.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: South Korean President Lee Jae-myung announced on June 30 a 1,350 trillion won ($880 billion) national investment plan spanning semiconductors, AI infrastructure, and robotics over the next 10 years. Samsung Group and SK Group will each build two new chip fabrication plants in the country's southwest, totaling roughly 800 trillion won in fab spending. A further 550 trillion won — including capital from Naver — will fund 8.4 gigawatts of AI data-center capacity by 2029. Lee announced the plan alongside the chairs of Samsung and SK Hynix in a televised address.

The rationale is not subtle. AI is memory-bound as much as it is compute-bound, and Samsung and SK Hynix already dominate the high-bandwidth memory that goes onto every leading-edge accelerator. The bet is that doubling manufacturing capacity while co-locating data centers keeps South Korea structurally essential to global AI infrastructure — even as the U.S. and China build out their own supply chains behind export controls.

This is the largest single national semiconductor investment announcement on record, and it lands in a run of comparable moves elsewhere: the U.S. CHIPS Act's ongoing extensions, the EU Chips Act's 2026 top-up, Japan's Rapidus subsidies, and India's fab incentives. What South Korea's plan makes plain is that AI infrastructure has now been re-categorized as national-survival capital-allocation — Lee framed it that way explicitly — closer to Cold War-era space or nuclear programs than to ordinary industrial policy.

For learners and early-career engineers: the labor demand implied by this plan is enormous, and much of it is not in model research. Fab process engineering, power systems, cooling, HBM packaging, data-center construction, and grid interconnection are the roles that scale a plan like this. If you are choosing where to spend the next five years, the interesting frontier is increasingly the physical layer that models run on — not just the models themselves.

sources.title: Lee's $880 Billion AI Bet Ties Legacy to South Korea Chip Boom — Bloomberg
